title: Flaky CI: New task-ordering tests assert non-deterministic order
problem: Five new tests added in a PR ~4 days ago intermittently fail in CI with ordering assertion errors. The tests pass locally (likely due to different timing, parallelism, or data ordering guarantees) but fail randomly in CI, blocking releases.
root_cause_hypothesis: The new TestTaskOrdering tests assert a specific element order that is not deterministically guaranteed by the underlying implementation. This is likely one of: (1) relying on iteration order of an unordered data structure (map/set), (2) asserting order on items with equal sort keys where tie-breaking is undefined, or (3) missing an explicit ORDER BY / sort call. The old tests either don't assert strict ordering or use deterministic inputs that avoid the ambiguity.
reproduction_steps: Identify the PR merged ~4 days ago that added 5 new task-ordering tests
Run the full test suite repeatedly (e.g., 20+ iterations or with -count=N flag) to reproduce the intermittent failure
Observe that only the new tests fail and only on some runs
environment: CI environment (specific runner not identified); failures do not reproduce locally, suggesting sensitivity to execution order, parallelism, or platform-level ordering differences
impact: Blocks all releases for the team. Every CI run has a chance of spurious failure, requiring manual re-runs and eroding trust in the test suite.
recommended_fix: Review the new tests from the identified PR. For each assertion that checks element ordering: (1) verify the production code guarantees that order — if not, either add an explicit sort/ORDER BY to the production code or change the test to use an order-independent assertion (e.g., assert set equality or sort both sides before comparing). Run the tests in a loop (50+ iterations) to confirm the fix eliminates flakiness.
proposed_test_case: A stress/repeat test that runs each of the new ordering tests 100 times in sequence and asserts zero failures, validating that the ordering assertions are deterministic.
information_gaps: Exact PR number and author (discoverable from git history)
Whether CI runs tests in parallel while local runs are sequential (could explain local-only pass)
Exact assertion style used in the new tests (string comparison vs set equality vs ordered list)
